title: Same-lap battlefield features plus weak undercut/overcut labels
summary: Official F1 strategy explanations are relative, not absolute: the tyre graphic compares cars in a battle, and Palmer reduces pit timing to undercut, overcut, and go-long archetypes.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explanations are relative, not absolute: the tyre graphic compares cars in a battle, and Palmer reduces pit timing to undercut, overcut, and go-long archetypes. The current best solutions are mostly single-car physics heuristics, so they likely miss whether a driver is strategically off-cycle versus the field.
implementation_hint: For each `Race x LapNumber`, compute ranks or percentiles of `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and stint age overall and within compound. Add counts of rivals who already stopped, are on older tyres, or sit on alternative strategies. Then weak-label historical pit laps as undercut/overcut/go-long from nearby-rival timing and train an auxiliary archetype model or use archetype probabilities as features.
expected_effect: Adds competitive-context signal and should better separate strategic cover stops from pure wear-driven stops.
risk: Same-lap aggregates are legal but easy to misalign; weak labels will be noisy and should be treated as soft targets.
